course.title: Lesson 25: Do the English Speak English

segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why does the writer not understand the porter?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么作者听不懂搬运工的话？

segments.text: I arrived in London at last.
The railway station was big, and dark.
I did not know the way to my hotel, so I asked a porter.

translations.zh-Hans: 我不知道去饭店的路，于是向一名搬运工打听。

segments.text: I not only spoke English very carefully, but very clearly as well.

translations.zh-Hans: 我不但非常认真地讲英语，而且咬字也很清楚。

segments.text: The porter, however, could not understand me.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，那搬运工却听不懂我的话。

segments.text: I repeated my question several times and at last he understood.

translations.zh-Hans: 我把问题重复了好几遍，他终于听懂了。

segments.text: He answered me, but he spoke neither slowly nor clearly.

translations.zh-Hans: 他回答了我，但既说得不慢，也说得不清楚。

segments.text: I am a foreigner, I said.
Then he spoke slowly, but I could not understand him.

translations.zh-Hans: 于是他说得慢了，可我还是听不懂。

segments.text: My teacher never spoke English like that.

translations.zh-Hans: 我的老师从来不那样讲英语。

segments.text: The porter and I looked at each other and smiled.

translations.zh-Hans: 搬运工和我互相看了看，都笑了。

segments.text: Then he said something and I understood it.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来他说了一句话，我听懂了。

segments.text: You'll soon learn English, he said.

translations.zh-Hans: “你很快就会学会英语的，”他说。

segments.text: In England, each person speaks a different language.

translations.zh-Hans: 在英国，每个人说的似乎都是一种不同的语言。

segments.text: The English understand each other, but I don't understand them.

translations.zh-Hans: 英国人彼此能听懂，可我听不懂他们。
